items.title: S&P 500 Earnings Will Soon Be Eclipsed. Blame Canada
items.content: US firms just put a lid on one of the best earnings seasons in years, posting 28% profit growth. They may be upstaged by their Canadian neighbors in the next reporting period as the first half comes to a close.
items.contentSnippet: US firms just put a lid on one of the best earnings seasons in years, posting 28% profit growth. They may be upstaged by their Canadian neighbors in the next reporting period as the first half comes to a close.
items.creator: Geoffrey Morgan, Stephanie Hughes

items.title: NYC ‘Classical Christian’ School Wins UWS Campus for $33 Million
items.content: Geneva School of Manhattan — an institution that describes itself as a “classical Christian school” — is acquiring the former Upper West Side home of a bankrupt private school for $33.2 million.
items.contentSnippet: Geneva School of Manhattan — an institution that describes itself as a “classical Christian school” — is acquiring the former Upper West Side home of a bankrupt private school for $33.2 million.
items.creator: Jonathan Randles

items.title: Fire Breaks Out at Delta Air Lines Refinery in Pennsylvania
items.content: A fire broke out at Delta Air Lines Inc.’s refinery in Trainer, Pennsylvania, on Thursday.
items.contentSnippet: A fire broke out at Delta Air Lines Inc.’s refinery in Trainer, Pennsylvania, on Thursday.
items.creator: Will Kubzansky

items.title: Ford CEO Says Human Workers Are Making a Difference
items.content: Ford Motor Co. Chief Executive Officer Jim Farley talks about how human workers are making all the difference on the factory floors. He speaks on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Ford Motor Co. Chief Executive Officer Jim Farley talks about how human workers are making all the difference on the factory floors. He speaks on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Treasuries Gain as Inflation Gauge Cools Fed Rate-Hike Bets
items.content: Treasuries gained after the Federal Reserve’s favored inflation gauge rose less than estimated, damping expectations for an interest-rate hike in the months ahead.
items.contentSnippet: Treasuries gained after the Federal Reserve’s favored inflation gauge rose less than estimated, damping expectations for an interest-rate hike in the months ahead.
items.creator: Ye Xie

items.title: Stocks Whipsaw as Micron Surges While Apple Sinks: Markets Wrap
items.content: A renewed wave of tech volatility gripped Wall Street, with a selloff in megacaps overshadowing gains in other corners of the stock market that were buoyed by signs the economy is in good shape.
items.contentSnippet: A renewed wave of tech volatility gripped Wall Street, with a selloff in megacaps overshadowing gains in other corners of the stock market that were buoyed by signs the economy is in good shape.
items.creator: Rita Nazareth

items.title: Figma Unveils Full-Stack Canvas for the AI Era
items.content: Collaborative design firm Figma unveiled a platform overhaul for the AI era at its annual Config conference in San Francisco, transforming its workspace into what it calls an "intelligent canvas" for full-stack digital creation. Figma co-founder and CEO Dylan Field joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Collaborative design firm Figma unveiled a platform overhaul for the AI era at its annual Config conference in San Francisco, transforming its workspace into what it calls an "intelligent canvas" for full-stack digital creation. Figma co-founder and CEO Dylan Field joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Darden Posts Weak Outlook as Olive Garden Sales Disappoint
items.content:
Darden Restaurants Inc. posted a cautious overall profit outlook as same-store sales at Olive Garden trailed expectations, raising questions about demand that overshadowed better-than-expected earnings.
Michael Halen, Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Restaurant and Foodservice Analyst, breaks down the earnings and gives his outlook for the broader food service industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

Darden Restaurants Inc. posted a cautious overall profit outlook as same-store sales at Olive Garden trailed expectations, raising questions about demand that overshadowed better-than-expected earnings.
Michael Halen, Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Restaurant and Foodservice Analyst, breaks down the earnings and gives his outlook for the broader food service industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Stellantis, Nissan in Talks to Buy Assets From Troubled Marelli
items.content: Stellantis NV and Nissan Motor Co. are in talks to take over some of the assets of Marelli Holdings Co., the global auto-parts maker that’s mired in a challenging reorganization in bankruptcy.
items.contentSnippet: Stellantis NV and Nissan Motor Co. are in talks to take over some of the assets of Marelli Holdings Co., the global auto-parts maker that’s mired in a challenging reorganization in bankruptcy.
items.creator: Alberto Brambilla, Giulia Morpurgo, Albertina Torsoli

items.title: H.B. Fuller Agrees to Acquire UK-Listed AMS for £659 Million
items.content: H.B. Fuller Co. agreed to acquire Advanced Medical Solutions Group Plc, a British maker of tissue-healing medical products, for £659 million ($870 million).
items.contentSnippet: H.B. Fuller Co. agreed to acquire Advanced Medical Solutions Group Plc, a British maker of tissue-healing medical products, for £659 million ($870 million).
items.creator: Liana Baker, Mengqi Sun

items.title: Hertz Heads for Record Weekly Drop on Funding Plan, Profit Guide
items.content: A record-setting rout in Hertz Global Holdings Inc. stock is continuing for a second day.
items.contentSnippet: A record-setting rout in Hertz Global Holdings Inc. stock is continuing for a second day.
items.creator: Jordan Fitzgerald

items.title: Michael Saylor’s Misfiring Funding Model Jolts Bitcoin Traders
items.content: Fears that Michael Saylor’s Bitcoin buying machine is beginning to seize up are spilling across the crypto market, fueling the latest leg of Bitcoin’s selloff and exposing cracks in one of the financial structures that has underpinned demand for the world’s largest cryptocurrency over the past two years.
items.contentSnippet: Fears that Michael Saylor’s Bitcoin buying machine is beginning to seize up are spilling across the crypto market, fueling the latest leg of Bitcoin’s selloff and exposing cracks in one of the financial structures that has underpinned demand for the world’s largest cryptocurrency over the past two years.
items.creator: Sidhartha Shukla

items.title: UNC-Chapel Hill Chancellor on Adding Students, Athletics
items.content: UNC-Chapel Hill Chancellor Lee Roberts discusses student growth, funding, college sports and a potential new stadium. He speaks with Romaine Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: UNC-Chapel Hill Chancellor Lee Roberts discusses student growth, funding, college sports and a potential new stadium. He speaks with Romaine Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Macflation Is Officially Here
items.content: Gadget prices inflate.
items.contentSnippet: Gadget prices inflate.
items.creator: Joe Weisenthal, Tracy Alloway

items.title: Ship Struck in Hormuz as Oil Supertankers Turn Back Again
items.content: A ship was hit by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after several freighters turned around while attempting to cross the vital waterway, developments that may undermine what had been a rapid reopening of the energy chokepoint.
items.contentSnippet: A ship was hit by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after several freighters turned around while attempting to cross the vital waterway, developments that may undermine what had been a rapid reopening of the energy chokepoint.
items.creator: Julian Lee, Alex Longley

items.title: Hetts: Ignore PEs, Watch Earnings
items.content: AI stocks may be stealing the spotlight, but Janus Henderson's Adam Hetts says investors are missing the bigger story. He joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why earnings, and not valuations, are driving markets, why sticky inflation isn't a dealbreaker, and why the rally is expanding well beyond Big Tech. (Source: Bloomberg)

He joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why earnings, and not valuations, are driving markets, why sticky inflation isn't a dealbreaker, and why the rally is expanding well beyond Big Tech. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Apple Shares Sink After Sweeping Price Hikes Hit iPads and Macs
items.content: Apple Inc. shares fell after it raised prices of all Macs, iPads, home devices and the Vision Pro on Thursday, seeking to offset cost hikes caused by an unprecedented shortage of memory chips and storage.
items.contentSnippet: Apple Inc. shares fell after it raised prices of all Macs, iPads, home devices and the Vision Pro on Thursday, seeking to offset cost hikes caused by an unprecedented shortage of memory chips and storage.
items.creator: Mark Gurman

items.title: Gold Steadies Near $4,000 as Traders Weigh Interest-Rate Outlook
items.content: Gold steadied after the release of US economic data, as traders slightly pulled back on expectations for interest rate hikes. Suki Cooper, Global Head: Commodities Research at Standard Chartered Bank, discusses her outlook on the precious metals space and why central banks are buying gold amid the pullback. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Gold steadied after the release of US economic data, as traders slightly pulled back on expectations for interest rate hikes. Suki Cooper, Global Head: Commodities Research at Standard Chartered Bank, discusses her outlook on the precious metals space and why central banks are buying gold amid the pullback. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Chipmakers Rally With Traders Piling In After Micron Results
items.content: Semiconductor stocks surged on Thursday as Micron Technology Inc.’s strong results underscored how artificial intelligence remains a major vector for growth.
items.contentSnippet: Semiconductor stocks surged on Thursday as Micron Technology Inc.’s strong results underscored how artificial intelligence remains a major vector for growth.
items.creator: Ryan Vlastelica, Subrat Patnaik

items.title: SpaceX Credit Derivatives Start to Trade After Debut Bond Sale
items.content: Credit-default swaps tied to Elon Musk’s SpaceX have begun actively trading after the company sold high-grade bonds this week for the first time, according to people familiar with the matter, allowing investors to hedge against potential losses or to speculate on the creditworthiness of the firm.
items.contentSnippet: Credit-default swaps tied to Elon Musk’s SpaceX have begun actively trading after the company sold high-grade bonds this week for the first time, according to people familiar with the matter, allowing investors to hedge against potential losses or to speculate on the creditworthiness of the firm.
items.creator: Caleb Mutua

items.title: China Raises €5 Billion in Record Euro Bond Sale
items.content: China sold €5 billion ($5.7 billion) of euro-denominated bonds, its biggest ever deal in the currency and the second in seven months.
items.contentSnippet: China sold €5 billion ($5.7 billion) of euro-denominated bonds, its biggest ever deal in the currency and the second in seven months.
items.creator: Janice Huang

items.title: US Consumer Spending Picks Up as Inflation Hits Three-Year High
items.content:
US consumer spending accelerated in May even as prices rose at the fastest pace in more than three years, suggesting Americans are powering through the fallout from the Iran war.
Rob Sockin, Chief US Economist at PGIM Credit, reacts to the PCE data and explains how the Fed might react to the numbers. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet:
US consumer spending accelerated in May even as prices rose at the fastest pace in more than three years, suggesting Americans are powering through the fallout from the Iran war.
Rob Sockin, Chief US Economist at PGIM Credit, reacts to the PCE data and explains how the Fed might react to the numbers. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Stocks Climb Even as Apple Price Hikes Offset Chipmaker Strength
items.content: Stocks solidified gains in the middle of a choppy trading session that saw Apple Inc.’s move to raise prices on its products unwinding some of the enthusiasm over strong chipmaker forecasts.
items.contentSnippet: Stocks solidified gains in the middle of a choppy trading session that saw Apple Inc.’s move to raise prices on its products unwinding some of the enthusiasm over strong chipmaker forecasts.
items.creator: Geoffrey Morgan, Jackie Edwards

items.title: Oil Rises as Traders Assess Pace of Hormuz Reopening, Safety
items.content: Oil rose as traders closely monitored shipments through the Strait of Hormuz following an incident involving a cargo ship off the Omani coast, renewing concerns about the safety of the key shipping corridor.
items.contentSnippet: Oil rose as traders closely monitored shipments through the Strait of Hormuz following an incident involving a cargo ship off the Omani coast, renewing concerns about the safety of the key shipping corridor.
items.creator: Yongchang Chin, Alex Longley

items.title: Bloomberg Surveillance 6/25/2026
items.content: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Hungary Yields Approach UK Gilts as Pro-EU Turn Fuels Rally
items.content: Hungary’s bonds have rallied so much that yields are approaching those in the UK, the latest sign that investors are backing the new government’s plans to fix the economy and join the euro region.
items.contentSnippet: Hungary’s bonds have rallied so much that yields are approaching those in the UK, the latest sign that investors are backing the new government’s plans to fix the economy and join the euro region.
items.creator: Kerim Karakaya, Andras Gergely, Srinivasan Sivabalan

items.title: Airfares Are Staying High Even as the Jet Fuel Crisis Fades
items.content: A historic jet fuel crisis is now fading as prices fall globally, but US passengers are yet to see the benefit with airfares still elevated.
items.contentSnippet: A historic jet fuel crisis is now fading as prices fall globally, but US passengers are yet to see the benefit with airfares still elevated.
items.creator: Will Kubzansky

items.title: Trump Requests $11.1 Billion in New Aid for Struggling Farmers
items.content: President Donald Trump had a surprise for the farmers he invited for dinner at the White House later Thursday: a request to Congress for $11.1 billion in assistance that would amount to the second bailout of the year for the beleaguered agriculture sector.
items.contentSnippet: President Donald Trump had a surprise for the farmers he invited for dinner at the White House later Thursday: a request to Congress for $11.1 billion in assistance that would amount to the second bailout of the year for the beleaguered agriculture sector.
items.creator: Michael Hirtzer, Erin Ailworth, Elizabeth Elkin

items.title: How the 1994 World Cup Changed the Business of Football Forever
items.content:
The World Cup had been an enormously popular event with surprisingly limited commercial significance; the 1990 tournament in Italy, for instance, lost money for broadcasters. Then the World Cup came to the US in 1994 and everything changed.

On this episode of the Odd Lots podcast, Joey D’Urso, author of More Than A Shirt, joins Tracy Alloway and Joe Weisenthal to discuss how advertisements and sponsorships by American corporations like McDonalds and General Motors in the 1994 World Cup changed the business of the sport forever.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: US Airline Stocks Erase Pandemic-Era Losses as Oil Prices Ease
items.content: A popular gauge of the US airline industry has finally recovered from pandemic-era losses after six years, as progress on a peace deal between US and Iran pushed oil prices lower and eased the pressure on carriers’ profitability.
items.contentSnippet: A popular gauge of the US airline industry has finally recovered from pandemic-era losses after six years, as progress on a peace deal between US and Iran pushed oil prices lower and eased the pressure on carriers’ profitability.
items.creator: Peyton Forte
